name: product-blueprint
description: Guided product-planning workflow from idea and research through concept, brand direction, PRD, cross-functional review, product definition, screen and service flows, and a design brief, with visual production available as a separate opt-in workflow.

interface.shortDescription: Turn an idea into an evidence-linked PRD, complete product flows, and a clear visual-design brief.
interface.longDescription: Product Blueprint guides research, product concept comparison, positioning and brand direction, PRD creation, a six-perspective evidence-linked review, explicit user decision gates, product definition, screen and service contracts, feasibility, low-fidelity flows, and a concise design brief. The default workflow stops before visual UI production. A separate opt-in design-production workflow validates comparable visual directions, earns approval on one representative screen, and only then expands to a design system, complete screens, prototype, and handoff. Validation checks structure and recorded evidence; it does not prove market demand, design taste, or implementation readiness.
defaultPrompt: Guide me from idea to an evidence-linked product plan: compare concepts, establish a brand direction, write and review the PRD, record explicit user decisions, confirm the first-version scope and product definition, map screens and flows, and finish with a visual-design brief. Stop before visual UI production unless I explicitly ask to continue with design-production.
